rules.name: 12th Lord Weak with Venus Aspect - Permanent Foreign Settlement
rules.description: When the 12th lord is weak and receives the aspect of Venus, the native permanently settles in a foreign land.

required.description: 12th lord must be in a weak state (debilitated, combust, or in enemy sign)

interpretation.summary: Permanent settlement in a foreign country is indicated when the 12th lord is weak and aspected by Venus.
interpretation.detail: Per Jataka Parijata 15:23, the weakness of the 12th lord severs ties to the homeland while Venus's aspect ensures the foreign settlement is comfortable and enduring.
interpretation.classical_context: Jataka Parijata emphasizes the 12th house as the house of foreign lands and expenditure. A weak lord indicates displacement from homeland.

rules.name: Moveable Lagna with Lord in Moveable Sign - Fortune Abroad
rules.description: When the Lagna is a moveable sign and its lord is placed in another moveable sign, the native finds fortune in foreign lands.

required.description: Lagna must be a moveable (cardinal) sign
Lord of Lagna must be placed in a moveable sign

interpretation.summary: Fortune and prosperity are found abroad when the Lagna is moveable and its lord occupies another moveable sign.
interpretation.detail: Per Jataka Parijata 15:26, the double moveable emphasis creates a powerful indication for travel and relocation that brings wealth and success in foreign lands.
interpretation.classical_context: Moveable signs (Aries, Cancer, Libra, Capricorn) are associated with movement, travel, and change of residence in classical Jyotish.

rules.name: Afflicted 12th House - Wandering Country to Country
rules.description: When the 12th house is afflicted by malefics, the native wanders from country to country without stable settlement.

required.description: 12th house must be afflicted by malefic planets (Saturn, Mars, Rahu, Ketu)

optional.description: 12th lord also weak or afflicted strengthens the indication

interpretation.summary: Affliction to the 12th house causes the native to wander restlessly from country to country.
interpretation.detail: Per BPHS 12:11, when malefics afflict the 12th house, the native cannot find stable settlement abroad and moves repeatedly between countries without finding a permanent home.
interpretation.classical_context: Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra identifies the 12th house as governing foreign lands, losses, and exile. Malefic affliction destabilizes any foreign residence.

rules.name: Lagna Lord in 12th + Malefic 10th + Moon-Mars - Exile/Wandering
rules.description: When the Lagna lord is in the 12th house, a malefic occupies the 10th house, and Moon-Mars conjunction exists, the native faces exile and wandering.

required.description: Lagna lord must be placed in the 12th house
A malefic planet must occupy the 10th house
Moon and Mars must be in conjunction

interpretation.summary: The combination of Lagna lord in 12th, malefic in 10th, and Moon-Mars conjunction produces exile and forced wandering.
interpretation.detail: Per Hora Sara v117 p268, this Abhi Yoga combination creates conditions where the native is forced into exile, wandering far from their homeland under difficult circumstances.
interpretation.classical_context: Hora Sara describes this as a yoga of displacement where the self (Lagna lord) is lost to foreign lands (12th), career (10th) is afflicted, and the mind-body (Moon-Mars) is agitated.

rules.name: Strong 12th Lord with Benefic Aspects - Beautiful Foreign Lands
rules.description: When the 12th lord is strong and receives aspects from benefic planets, the native sojourns in beautiful foreign lands.

required.description: 12th lord must be strong (exalted, in own sign, or in friendly sign)
Benefic planets must aspect the 12th lord

interpretation.summary: A strong 12th lord with benefic aspects indicates sojourning in beautiful and pleasant foreign lands.
interpretation.detail: Per Jataka Parijata 15:24, when the 12th lord is dignified and blessed by benefic aspects, the native's foreign experiences are luxurious, pleasant, and in aesthetically beautiful locations.
interpretation.classical_context: The strength of the 12th lord determines the quality of foreign experience. Benefic aspects ensure comfort, beauty, and positive outcomes in foreign lands.

rules.name: Saturn 1st + Mars 12th + Sun/Moon/Mercury 5th - Foreign Death
rules.description: When Saturn is in the 1st house, Mars in the 12th house, and Sun/Moon/Mercury in the 5th house, the native dies in a foreign land or temple.

required.description: Saturn must be placed in the 1st house (Lagna)
Mars must be placed in the 12th house
Sun, Moon, and/or Mercury must be in the 5th house

interpretation.summary: This combination indicates the native will permanently settle abroad and ultimately die in a foreign land or sacred place.
interpretation.detail: Per Jataka Parijata v109, Saturn in the 1st restricts the native's connection to homeland, Mars in the 12th drives them to foreign lands through conflict or necessity, and the luminaries/Mercury in the 5th disconnect them from their native culture and progeny.
interpretation.classical_context: This is a rare and specific yoga indicating permanent displacement ending in death abroad. The 'temple' reference suggests death may occur at a pilgrimage or sacred site far from home.

rules.name: Rahu in 9th House - Foreign Guru/Religion
rules.description: When Rahu is placed in the 9th house, the native is drawn to foreign gurus, religions, or spiritual systems different from their birth tradition.

required.description: Rahu must be placed in the 9th house

optional.description: Rahu in a strong sign amplifies the foreign spiritual pull

interpretation.summary: Rahu in the 9th house draws the native toward foreign gurus, religions, and spiritual traditions outside their birth culture.
interpretation.detail: Per Nirayana System p169, Rahu in the 9th house creates fascination with foreign philosophies and religions. The native may convert to a foreign religion, follow a foreign guru, or relocate to pursue spiritual knowledge from an alien tradition.
interpretation.classical_context: The 9th house governs dharma, guru, and higher learning. Rahu's placement here creates an unconventional or foreign approach to these matters.

rules.name: Ketu in 9th House - Wandering Mendicant
rules.description: When Ketu is placed in the 9th house, the native becomes a wandering mendicant or spiritual seeker who travels without fixed residence.

required.description: Ketu must be placed in the 9th house

optional.description: Association with Saturn or 12th lord intensifies the wandering pattern

interpretation.summary: Ketu in the 9th house produces a wandering mendicant pattern where the native travels without stable residence.
interpretation.detail: Per Nirayana System p169, Ketu in the 9th house detaches the native from conventional dharma and homeland roots, producing a wandering mendicant who seeks spiritual liberation through travel and renunciation of fixed abode.
interpretation.classical_context: Ketu represents detachment and liberation. In the 9th house of dharma and long journeys, it creates a pattern of perpetual spiritual wandering and renunciation of worldly settlement.
